description: Centralised (non-federated) training baseline on the pooled DermaMNIST training set. Single seed; serves as the upper-bound anchor for the FL comparison.

federation_tax.description: Centralised macro-F1 minus federated macro-F1; how much performance is lost by federating instead of pooling data.
federation_tax.interpretation: FedProx recovers 91.5% of centralised macro-F1 vs FedAvg's 86.7%, closing 36% of the gap between FedAvg and the centralised upper bound.

caveats: Single seed for centralised — across-seed variance not quantified. Comparisons to FL means are descriptive, not statistically inferential.
Convergence at epoch 48 of 50 — model was still improving at wall-clock cutoff. A 100-epoch run might push centralised macro-F1 to ~0.58–0.60.

FedProx-on-actinic appears to exceed centralised-on-actinic (0.471 vs 0.433), but actinic has only ~67 test samples; this difference is within seed noise and should not be interpreted as FL beating centralised.
